Mio Yasutake created a sample repository that demonstrates various Fiori elements navigation patterns along with their actual UI behavior. See how.

Events

SAP UX Innovation Days are back for 2026

SAP UX Innovation Day Silicon Valley – Thursday, June 11

Join us at SAP UX Innovation Day Silicon Valley to learn about the intersection between AI and user experience. Our goal is to empower you with knowledge on how to build SAP Fiori apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and all the new AI functionality that we are building into the SAP Fiori elements framework. We will cover AI for business users and AI-powered tools for application developers.

Register and more details.

Mark your calendar for other SAP UX Innovation Days in your region:

Dublin, Ireland - Wednesday, September 30

Walldorf, Germany - Thursday, December 10

Other Events

Sapphire – lots of great UX sessions

UI5con - A vibrant gathering for UI5 enthusiasts to connect, learn, and innovate

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori Development newsletter March 2026 (issue #39)

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2026 (issue #38)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2025 (issue #37)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2025 (issue #36)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2025 (issue #35)

SAP FIori development newsletter May 2025 (Issue #34)

entry.title: AI in SAP: Where Value Is Actually Being Created
entry.content:
Everybody has been talking about the massive transformations that we are witnessing in regards AI and enterprise systems. I'm not saying this is not true, I've seen it myself, especially in industries like energy, where operations are becoming more data-driven every day. In many ways, the impact is already there. My question is: where exactly is AI making a difference?

Because even though technology is moving fast, the impact doesn’t always show up at the same pace. In most cases, the problem isn’t whether AI works or not, because it usually does. The real challenge is what happens after the model produces an output, and whether that output actually turns into something the business can act on.

From what we’ve seen in different projects, many organizations are already experimenting with AI across their SAP landscape. Individual use cases are delivering valuable results that go from producing accurate outputs to outperforming traditional approaches. What happens when companies try to take that value beyond a single use case and extend it across processes? Do things start to get complicated?

The challenge usually doesn’t show up when the model is running. It appears a bit later, when teams try to use those outputs in every day operations. Insights might be accurate, but that doesn’t automatically mean they fit into how the business actually works. Maybe, instead of asking ourselves if something works, we should shift the conversation to how to actually use it in a consistent way. 

Fitting a model or an insight into a complex process that already exists is the real challenge. Most companies do not really have issues in generating insights: They can forecast better, process information faster, and detect patterns that were hard to see before. But what comes after? What happens once the insight is there?

In an SAP environment, acting on that insight is rarely a simple step. Even something as straightforward as improving a forecast can have countless effects across different areas. Adjusting inventory levels or procurement volumes doesn’t stay within a single function. It impacts planning, finance, logistics, and sometimes even customer commitments. Making that change actually happen requires aligned data, coordinated processes, and systems that can absorb the adjustment without breaking.

We’ve seen this take place very clearly in our work with a global energy company. The challenge there wasn’t a lack of data or visibility, but how to act on it in a way that was both fast and reliable. Inspecting thousands of kilometers of transmission lines manually was slow, risky, and difficult to scale. By introducing drones combined with AI-based image recognition, they were able to detect potential issues much earlier and speed up decision-making around maintenance.

 

Although the model identifying anomalies made a really big difference, it was how that information connected back into their SAP processes that changed everything. The insights generated in the field needed to trigger actions like maintenance planning and work order creation, without adding more manual steps. Once that connection was in place, the impact became much more tangible: inspections became significantly safer, productivity increased, and response times improved.

This is where a pattern starts to emerge. AI is already delivering value, but mostly in scenarios where the scope is controlled and the dependencies are limited. The challenge appears when organizations try to move beyond those contained use cases and bring AI into core operations.

At that point, the underlying SAP architecture starts to matter a lot more. Data consistency, integration, and process standardization are no longer background topics. They become the difference between an insight that looks good on paper and one that actually drives action. The more companies rely on AI-generated outputs, the more they depend on their systems being able to respond without friction.

What we’ve learned from these experiences is that the shift doesn’t happen when you add more models. It happens when those models become part of how processes run end to end. That’s when AI stops being something experimental and starts becoming part of everyday operations.

So, to answer my initial question: value is not created when a model detects an anomaly or improves a forecast. It’s created when that output leads to a decision that is actually executed. And in SAP environments, that step from insight to action is still where most of the real work is happening today.

AI is already showing what’s possible. But the organizations that will see the biggest impact are the ones that can connect that potential to how their systems actually run.

entry.title: Something Exciting is on the Horizon: Your Sapphire 2026 Session Guide
entry.content:
If you have not already, check out Mark Wright's blog post on What to Expect from AI Agents and Agentic Development — it sets the stage perfectly for what is coming.

Sapphire 2026  is shaping up to be one of the most exciting yet. This year, we will be unveiling a wave of new AI features that our team has been developing behind the scenes. We cannot wait to share them with you.

Whether you are just getting started with SAP Build and Joule Studio or you are already an expert, there is something here for you. Our sessions are designed to meet you where you are — giving you the insights, demos, and hands-on learning to take your skills to the next level.

Join us at Sapphire 2026 — register for the format that works best for you:

  • SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference Orlando
  • SAP Sapphire Virtual
  • SAP Sapphire Madrid

 

If you are planning to visit SAP Sapphire in person, stop by our live event demo booth to meet the team and see innovation in action. Discover how SAP Build helps teams create AI-powered solutions—from extensions to workflows and agentic apps—so you can deliver faster with more agility and less complexity. Do not miss our Agent Lab experience, where you can take part in a hands-on session and see just how easy it is to build AI agents that streamline and simplify your daily work:

  • Orlando station (BTP2301) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP2788)
  • Madrid station (BTP1408) and Agent Lab Experience Hub (EXP1537)

 

To help you plan your agenda, we have also curated a selection of must-attend sessions. These highlight the latest AI capabilities for agent-based applications and workflow development, bringing them to life through real-world examples.

One session, however, stands out as essential: “The Enterprise Path to Agentic AI” (BTP1454). Presented by Michael Gall, Sid Misra, and Corey Swenson, this session explores how Joule Studio enables the creation of AI solutions ranging from simple extensions to fully agentic applications. It also highlights SAP’s strategy for delivering an enterprise-grade, intent-to-execution platform tailored for SAP customers. Attendees will see how the Joule solution provides a unified, guided experience — leading developers from initial intent through blueprinting and agent specification to testing and deployment.

entry.title: Adapting SAP Joule for Purchase Requisition Creation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this blog post!

As a member of the SAP S/4HANA RIG (Regional Implementation Group), our mission is to share collective insights and hands‑on experience with the broader SAP community through blog posts and SAP Learning courses. To date, we have published more than 700 blog posts and delivered numerous learning offerings to help customers successfully navigate SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

Today, I would like to show how you can leverage SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA and, more importantly, how you can adapt it to your specific business needs. While this example focuses on Purchase Requisition (PR) creation, the same principles can be applied to many other business processes.

SAP Joule and Purchase Requisition Creation

As you can see in the video below, as of May 2026, Joule can guide an end user through the creation of a Purchase Requisition. Additional details are available in the SAP Help Portal here: Create Purchase Requisitions.

 

This capability is already very useful for many customers. However, we also know that every customer has unique requirements. After presenting this process, I frequently received the following questions:

  • Can Joule create a Purchase Requisition using our own document type?
  • Can Joule populate additional standard fields, such as the PR Description?
  • I created a custom field in the F1481 - Custom fields app and enabled it in the respective Purchase requisition API. Could that custom field be included in the prompt?
  • Our team only creates PRs for materials. Can Joule assume I will request a material and skip the initial question?
  • Is it possible to request a PR that includes all the required information in a single prompt?
  • Can multiple materials be included directly during PR creation, rather than as a follow‑up step?

The short answer to all of these questions is yes—by leveraging Joule Studio through Agents and Skills.

As part of the SAP Build portfolio, Joule Studio allows you to tailor Joule to your specific business requirements using an intuitive, user-friendly interface. It enables you to extend standard capabilities, connect to existing SAP and customer-specific services, and guide how Joule responds based on business context and input.

In addition, Joule Studio lets you define AI agents with clear roles and areas of focus, ensuring Joule behaves in a way that aligns with your processes and supports users more effectively in their daily work.

And rather than just taking my word for it, you can see it in action in the following video:

For this case, I used a single Prompt:

“Using Easy Purchaser, create a PR 'ZNB', "Blog Joule", with: TG11, plant: 1710, 5 PC, date: 01/06/2026; TG12, plant: 1710, 7 PC, date: 01/06/2026, ZJoule: Demo”.

Compared with the initial video, Joule did not ask for a source of supply or whether the request was for a material or a service. It also allowed me to select the document type “ZNB”, populate the PR description, and fill the custom field “ZJoule.” This behaviour is intentional and reflects how Joule was configured in Joule Studio to align with the business process and reduce unnecessary user input.

 

Key Takeaway

SAP delivers a standard experience for Joule‑driven object creation. At the same time, you remain in control. By leveraging SAP Joule Studio, you can tailor Joule to meet your organization’s unique needs—without being limited to a fixed, predefined flow.

 

The SAP S/4HANA RIG Team is preparing a series of technical and functional blogs to walk you through some examples. The next blog will focus on the technical steps to enable this scenario and to adapt the Joule Agent to your business needs. Links will be updated here. Stay tuned!

Thank you again for reading, and I encourage you to embrace the latest innovations that come with SAP S/4HANA. If you found this blog helpful, please consider giving it a Like or sharing it with others.

I would also love to hear your thoughts and feedback—feel free to leave a comment. If you have any questions, please post them in the Q&A section using the tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. We will do our best to respond as quickly as possible.

 

Thanks,
Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG Team
